title: Have Archaeologists MISSED an Important Chapter of Human History?

transcript.text: so much of our planet has not really
been subjected to detailed intensive
archaeological survey I think it's
extremely premature for archaeologists
to be so confident in their model of the
origins of civilization they should have
some humility and allow some leg room
for alternative suggestions to come in
instead of just trying to smash down
anybody who says something that doesn't
agree with their model must be just a
little more warm-hearted a little more
open yeah allow that and and see what's
good in the in the alternative evidence
is being presented rather than just
trying to find bad things about it and
have a more constructive dialogue
instead of this you know we know best
and we're going to smash you down and
we're going to insult you and humiliate
you and call you any names we can so
childish really go back that's been
discovered since the 90s yeah it feels
like kind of overwhelming that the
consensus should actually shift to yes
at the very least there should be
willingness to consider the possibility
that we have missed an important chapter
of the human story
